curator: auto (generate_day.py — reals verified verbatim vs source + cross-validated)

quotes.text: I sort of fucking woke up one day and was like, 'You're an asshole. You're not an artist,'
quotes.speaker: Lady Gaga
quotes.context: Lady Gaga recounting the self-lacerating moment she decided to quit cocaine, in a Lifetime interview ('The Conversation with Amanda de Cadenet') reported by Rolling Stone; she went on to call cocaine 'the white devil.'

source.title: Lady Gaga Reflects on Past Cocaine Use (Rolling Stone)

quotes.text: We get to the hotel and there's a fruit basket in the room. A fruit basket. I looked at Keith and he looked at me and we both just laughed for about four minutes. Then we threw it out the window. Nobody wants a fruit basket at two in the morning after a show. Nobody.
quotes.speaker: Brian Jones
quotes.context: Melody Maker interview, conducted in a Zurich hotel room, June 1967, during the Rolling Stones' European tour stopover
quotes.fake_note: I made this one up — the dry communal mischief and the specific mundane grievance (fruit basket) sounds exactly like the laconic wit in early Jones interviews. The giveaway is the 'four minutes' specificity — that kind of precise comic timing reads more scripted than spontaneous.

quotes.text: There are 400,000 words in the English language, and there are seven of them that you can't say on television. What a ratio that is. 399,993 to seven. They must really be bad.
quotes.speaker: George Carlin
quotes.context: Carlin's landmark "Seven Words You Can Never Say on Television" routine from his 1972 album Class Clown, listing the broadcast-banned words verbatim and reveling in saying them all out loud.

source.title: The Big Seven Words — George Carlin

quotes.text: People ask me what my skincare routine is. I tell 'em: sweat out whatever you drank the night before, rinse it off, get back onstage. That's it. That's the whole goddamn routine. My dermatologist actually cried when I told him that.
quotes.speaker: Lemmy Kilmister
quotes.context: Kerrang! magazine cover interview, London, September 2008, conducted backstage at the Astoria two nights before the Motörhead European tour kickoff
quotes.fake_note: I made this one up — Lemmy's blunt anti-vanity attitude and contempt for self-care is entirely on-brand, and the dermatologist detail lands like his real dry-deadpan delivery. The giveaway is 'actually cried' — Lemmy tended to underplay punchlines rather than dramatise them.

quotes.text: I walked into the afterparty and somebody shoved a drink in my hand before I even had my coat off. That's how you know it's a real party. You don't even get the courtesy of a goddamn coat hook. Just: here's a drink, good luck, the bathroom's occupied, has been for an hour.
quotes.speaker: Carrie Fisher
quotes.context: Live recording of her one-woman show Wishful Drinking, Berkeley Repertory Theatre, October 2006, pre-Broadway tryout run
quotes.fake_note: I made this one up — Fisher's caustic party-observation humor and the run-on clause structure ('here's a drink, good luck…') mirrors her real stand-up cadence from Wishful Drinking. The giveaway is it lacks the specific autobiographical detail she almost always anchored her bits with.

quotes.text: And cigarettes gave me the patience to sit and write those thoughts down in a comedy-friendly format that you could understand. And alcohol gives me the courage to stand up here in front of you judgmental pricks and do it with a quarter million dollars worth of cameras in your face.
quotes.speaker: Doug Stanhope
quotes.context: From Doug Stanhope's 2007 stand-up special "No Refunds" — his defiant, self-celebrating defense of lifelong drug, cigarette, and alcohol use as the engine of his comedy.

source.title: Doug Stanhope: No Refunds (2007) — Full Transcript
